name: Miron Sadziak

experiences.title: Engineering Manager & VP, Inventory Management, Global Markets
Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription: Developed an Object Relational Mapping module for abstracting data access inside the Java EE/WebSphere-based framework which is now used for Bank of Japan for communication with market participants.

experiences.title: Software Developer
experiences.jobDescription:
Created a 3D visualization module based on open source toolkit VTK, for an electromagnetic simulator working on a multi-processor supercomputer. Developed an application in Java using Swing for accessing data gathered in experiments, visualizing them as interactive graphs and performing calculations on them. Created a few smaller programs in Java and C++ for conversion and edition of XML files containing experiment definitions and results.

Working with C++, Java, XML, OpenGL, MPI, Fortran

recommendations.name: Ashutosh Gupta
recommendations.subtitle: Ex Goldman Sachs | Director | Architect | Capital Markets & FinTech Leader | AWS Certified | Driving Innovation, Digital Transformation & Operational Excellence | RegTech | Collateral Management
recommendations.context: Miron worked with Ashutosh on the same team
recommendations.description:
I worked with Ashutosh at Goldman Sachs, where he consistently demonstrated strong leadership and technical expertise. He effectively managed complex projects across global teams and translated business challenges into scalable, efficient solutions.

Ashutosh applies sharp analytical thinking and deep technical skills to every initiative. His ability to foster collaboration across regions and drive results through clear strategy and execution makes him a valuable contributor in any environment.

He is a thoughtful leader and a skilled technologist. Any team would benefit from his experience and dedication.

recommendations.name: Rob Cox
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Software Engineer at Jump
recommendations.context: Rob was senior to Miron but didn't manage Miron directly
recommendations.description:
I had the privilege of working with Rob during our time at Goldman Sachs, and he consistently demonstrated excellence across every dimension of software engineering and team leadership.

Rob’s technical depth is remarkable. He writes clean, well-tested code and applies a strong foundation in computer science to every challenge. His solutions are functionally effective, pragmatically designed, and architecturally resilient - built with a clear focus on solving the business problem while ensuring long-term scalability and robustness.

He’s also a standout collaborator. Rob worked seamlessly across globally distributed teams, fostering alignment and clarity across time zones and cultures. His ability to connect with business users and translate complex requirements into elegant technical solutions made him an invaluable bridge between technology and finance.

Rob’s domain knowledge in capital markets, trading systems, and fixed income is extensive. He doesn’t settle for surface-level understanding - he dives deep into the business problem and ensures the solution is both technically sound and strategically aligned.

As a leader, Rob sets the tone for excellence. He mentors teammates with patience and insight, helping others grow while driving the team forward. I learned a great deal from working with him, and I’m grateful for the opportunity to have had him as a role model and collaborator.

recommendations.name: Marcuz Corpuz
recommendations.subtitle: Software Engineer | I build fast, reliable web apps for travel & hospitality
recommendations.description: I had the privilege of managing Marcuz Corpuz during our time at Goldman Sachs Japan, where his frontend expertise consistently stood out. Marcuz has a rare talent for crafting intuitive, high-performance user interfaces, blending design sensibility with deep technical fluency in JavaScript, TypeScript, and React.js. His work was always grounded in a strong understanding of user needs and system constraints, resulting in clean, efficient solutions. Beyond the frontend, Marcuz was equally capable across the stack—jumping into backend development when needed and consistently producing high-quality, maintainable code. He’s a meticulous problem-solver, able to troubleshoot and debug complex issues with clarity and precision. Marcuz also excelled in global coordination, working seamlessly with teams across regions and time zones. His collaborative spirit and clear communication made him a key bridge between stakeholders, ensuring alignment and momentum even in complex, cross-border initiatives.

recommendations.name: Henry Mugasha
recommendations.subtitle: Software Engineer Goldman Sachs
recommendations.context: Miron worked with Henry on the same team
recommendations.description:
I had the opportunity to work with Henry Mugasha at Goldman Sachs, and I was consistently impressed by his technical depth, professionalism, and collaborative spirit.

Henry has strong technical skills, with deep expertise in Java, distributed architectures, and microservices. He’s also highly proficient in SQL, Sybase, and Spring, and brings a thoughtful, analytical approach to every problem he tackles. Whether debugging complex issues under pressure or designing scalable systems, Henry consistently delivers high-quality solutions.

He’s a great team player, and works seamlessly within globally distributed teams, always ensuring smooth coordination and clear communication. Henry builds strong relationships with business users and stakeholders, taking the time to understand their workflows and challenges. His ability to translate those insights into efficient, well-targeted solutions is one of his standout strengths.

Henry’s communication skills are excellent—he’s clear, responsive, and always focused on driving progress. Working with him was a pleasure, and I’d highly recommend him to any team looking for a technically strong, business-savvy, and collaborative engineer.

recommendations.name: Haris Lazanitis
recommendations.subtitle: Co-Founder at palinor.io | CTO at G20 | ex-Citadel | ex-Goldman Sachs
recommendations.description:
I had the privilege of working under Haris Lazanitis during our time at Goldman Sachs Japan, and I can confidently say Haris is one of the most exceptional engineering leaders I’ve worked with.
Haris brings deep technical expertise and strong coding skills, consistently designing systems that are efficient, performant, and maintainable. His ability to troubleshoot complex problems is unmatched - he approaches challenges with clarity and precision, often resolving issues that others struggle with.
Beyond his engineering strengths, Haris has a remarkable ability to connect with business users, understand their needs, and deliver strategic, effective solutions. His communication skills and team-oriented mindset make him a natural collaborator, and he’s highly effective at coordinating across regions to keep teams aligned and productive.
As a manager, Haris provides the right level of autonomy, while offering strong oversight and mentorship. He’s deeply invested in the growth of his team, and his leadership style fosters trust, innovation, and excellence.
I’m grateful for the opportunity to have worked with Haris and highly recommend him to any organization looking for a strategic, technically brilliant, and people-first leader.

recommendations.name: Himanshu Makkar
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Software Engineer at Goldman Sachs | ex - Amazon, Samsung | IIT Roorkee
recommendations.context: Miron was senior to Himanshu but didn't manage Himanshu directly
recommendations.description: I had the pleasure of working with Himanshu Makkar on multiple projects at Goldman Sachs, and his thoughtful, user-focused approach consistently stood out. Himanshu cares deeply about delivering the right solution for the business, taking time to understand the problem space through close collaboration with stakeholders and users. He’s highly conscientious, writing clean, well-tested code and maintaining a strong commitment to quality throughout the development lifecycle. His troubleshooting skills are sharp, and his ability to navigate complex production issues is matched by his calm, supportive presence on the team. Himanshu communicates clearly, builds trust across groups, and brings a collaborative energy that makes him both approachable and highly effective. Working with him was always smooth, insightful, and outcome-driven.

recommendations.name: Steven Doogan
recommendations.subtitle: Executive Director at Goldman Sachs
recommendations.context: Miron worked with Steven on the same team
recommendations.description: I had the opportunity to work with Steven Doogan on multiple initiatives at Goldman Sachs, and his leadership and technical depth consistently stood out. Steven combines deep domain expertise in the funding area with a strong command of optimization algorithms - particularly linear programming - as well as exceptional technical and system design skills, applying this comprehensive toolkit to deliver robust, scalable, and effective systems. He writes high-quality, well-tested code and brings sharp troubleshooting skills to even the most complex challenges. Steven is a strong team player with outstanding communication skills, able to coordinate seamlessly across teams and regions, and lead globally dispersed engineering groups with clarity and confidence. His ability to align technical solutions with business needs makes him a trusted and highly impactful contributor.

recommendations.name: Bharat Reddy
recommendations.subtitle: Software Engineer @ Snowflake ❄️
recommendations.context: Miron was senior to Bharat but didn't manage Bharat directly
recommendations.description: I had the pleasure of working with Bharat Reddy on multiple projects at Goldman Sachs, and I was consistently impressed by his technical depth, reliability, and collaborative spirit. Bharat writes high-quality, well-tested code and brings strong troubleshooting skills to even the most complex challenges. He’s a dependable team player who leads projects with precision, consistently delivering on time and with great attention to detail. Bharat communicates clearly, coordinates effectively across regions, and works closely with business stakeholders and users to ensure the solutions he delivers are tailored, impactful, and aligned with real-world needs.

recommendations.name: Shohei Toyama
recommendations.subtitle: null
recommendations.description: I had the pleasure of managing Shohei Toyama at Goldman Sachs Tokyo, where he consistently demonstrated deep technical expertise, conscientious attention to detail, and a strong commitment to delivering high-quality solutions. Shohei is both a reliable team player and an independent contributor, able to coordinate effectively across diverse stakeholder groups while keeping user experience at the forefront of his thinking. His ability to reach unmatched levels of domain expertise—and apply that knowledge directly to the solutions he builds—makes him an invaluable asset to any engineering team.

recommendations.name: Mizuki Maruno
recommendations.subtitle: Global Markets Engineering
recommendations.description: I had the pleasure of working with Mizuki Maruno at Goldman Sachs Tokyo, where she consistently demonstrated exceptional communication skills, conscientiousness, and a deep commitment to understanding business needs. Mizuki builds strong, trust-based relationships with stakeholders and users, quickly grasps new concepts, and consistently proposes thoughtful, well-grounded solutions. She's a reliable team player with a calm, collaborative style that makes her a valued contributor across both technical and business domains.

recommendations.name: Garima Dahiya
recommendations.subtitle: VP, Goldman Sachs | PhD, TiTech | IIT Mandi | Miranda House
recommendations.description: I had the pleasure of managing Garima Dahiya at Goldman Sachs Tokyo, where she consistently impressed me with her sharp mathematical thinking, strong coding and troubleshooting abilities, and proactive approach to problem-solving. She quickly grasps new concepts, communicates clearly, and always seeks to understand the broader business context before proposing thoughtful, effective solutions. Whether collaborating with teammates or driving initiatives independently, Garima demonstrates leadership, reliability, and a genuine commitment to excellence.

recommendations.name: Krishna Prakash.K
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Technology Analyst | Production Incident Management
recommendations.description: I have been working with Krishna Prakash for many years, and it has always been a pleasure. He performs his role at top level, with great diligence and passion. He has strong technical and problem solving skills, maintains deep knowledge of the systems and areas we support, and has helped us many times get out of trouble. He is a great team player, communicates effectively with both engineering and business stakeholders, and always delivers what he is entrusted with. I'm confident he can deliver top performance in every role and environment.

recommendations.name: Jordi Segura
recommendations.subtitle: Digital Transformation & Product Leadership | Driving Innovation in Energy & Technology | 17+ Years Modernizing Global Operations
recommendations.context: Miron worked with Jordi but on different teams
recommendations.description: Jordi is very sharp and has extensive knowledge in areas of electronics and computer engineering. He is eager to learn, dependable and professional. His knowledge of languages, communication skills and openness make him perfectly fit to work in multicultural environments.

summary: Miron Sadziak is currently a member of Microsoft's Industry Solutions Engineering (ISE) team within a large enterprise technology organization. His posts primarily focus on recruitment and team-building for roles like Technical Program Manager (TPM), Principal/ Senior Data Scientist, Principal Architect and Principal Software Engineer, emphasizing Tokyo-based hiring and global collaboration. He consistently highlights cross-functional, co-development work with customers—bringing designers, engineers, data scientists and TPMs together to build real-world products using the latest AI technologies. His tone is professional, enthusiastic and practical, mixing clear job and career information, resource links (job descriptions and blog posts), and bilingual outreach (Japanese and English) to engage local and global candidates. Miron emphasizes impact-oriented work: solving industry-specific problems with AI, close alignment with product teams, and transforming customer development culture. Posts show a recruiting/community-builder pattern—frequent job announcements, invitations to connect or ask questions, and occasional event promotion—positioning him as a connector between talent and enterprise AI product opportunities.
